excerpt: There is a life force that exists in a hidden state throughout the world. When the darkness of the spiritual realm descended, it fell into the form of substance and shape.
The self is singular in essence, yet its forces emerge at different times and stations. When the fetus possesses only the power to grow, it is named the vegetative soul.
source_location_hint: Classification of souls by developmental stage

excerpt: Perfection cannot be achieved without inner knowledge, and all acts of the Shariah are rooted in the guardianship of the Commander of the Faithful. Wilayah is the soul of all action and knowledge.
The vegetative soul possesses four natures, corresponding to four humors—black bile, yellow bile, phlegm, and blood—at four centers called the mothers.
source_location_hint: Humoral theory in relation to vegetative soul

excerpt: The food of the sensible soul is knowledge. The Prophet declared, 'If a believer is deprived of the company of scholars for forty days, his soul begins to grow toward corruption.'
source_location_hint: Spiritual nourishment and the role of teachers

excerpt: It is the Almighty who grants substance and support to the believer whose chain of transmission proceeds from the First Intellect.
source_location_hint: Connection of human soul to primordial intellect

excerpt: The universal soul is an ocean, and what the human receives is a small wave from that ocean. The rational soul endeavors to make the sensible soul resemble itself.

When all four souls attain their perfection, all become and remain in one form—a single light. This process reaches the Gate of Gates and through the established leaders descends finally to the whole congregation.
The sensible soul pervades all things of creation and remains inward through concealment, yet manifests through action. It is present in minerals, vegetation, animals, and humans in graduated forms.
source_location_hint: Universal distribution of sensible soul across creation
